title: QuickBooks Online Lab
subhead: QuickBooks Online is the #1 accounting software requested in real entry-level job postings. This module sets up Skarion Manufacturing LLC from scratch and walks through the workflows you'll use every single day on the job.
hook: 45% of entry-level accounting postings explicitly require QuickBooks experience. This is the software you'll actually be judged on in your first interview.

chunks.title: This lesson pairs with a live QuickBooks Online trial
chunks.bodyHtml:
Everything in this module is designed to be followed inside a real, free QuickBooks Online trial (30 days, no credit card required for the trial). The company you'll build is Skarion Manufacturing LLC — US locale, USD, Manassas, Virginia.

The walkthrough script and every dollar figure below are real numbers pulled directly from the rest of the course (the January practice packet, the AP invoice batch, the bank statements) — not made up, and not from an unrelated demo company. Where you see a bracketed [SCREENSHOT] marker, that's where you'd capture your own screen while following along live.

callout.html: Heads up: an earlier draft of this walkthrough was built inside the wrong QuickBooks demo company ("kaizen", a different currency, unrelated numbers). It's been fully corrected here to use real Skarion data throughout.

chunks.title: Company setup, chart of accounts, opening balances
chunks.bodyHtml:
1. Company setup: QBO free trial → United States/USD → Company name: Skarion Manufacturing LLC → Industry: Manufacturing → Fiscal year Jan–Dec → Accrual basis.

2. Chart of accounts — the core accounts you'll use throughout the course:

TypeKey accounts
Assets1000 Operating Checking · 1100 Accounts Receivable · 1500 Fixed Assets · 1590 Accumulated Depreciation
Liabilities2000 Accounts Payable · 2100 Sales Tax Payable · 2500 Loan Payable
Revenue/COGS4000 Sales Revenue · 5000 Cost of Goods Sold
Expenses6100 Rent · 6300 Wages · 6700 Depreciation · 6800 Interest

3. Opening balances (January 1, 2026): Operating Checking $132,500.00 · Accounts Receivable $118,400.00 · Accounts Payable $82,300.00 · Loan Payable $250,000.00.

chunks.title: QBO navigation — know where things live

cards.front: Where do you enter a vendor bill?
cards.back: Expenses > Bills — this is the AP entry point.

cards.front: Where do you create a customer invoice?
cards.back: Sales > Invoices — this is the AR entry point.

cards.front: Where do you reconcile a bank account?
cards.back: Accounting > Reconcile (or the gear icon > Tools > Reconcile).

cards.front: Where do you run the P&L and Balance Sheet?
cards.back: Reports > search "Profit and Loss" or "Balance Sheet" — both export to Excel from the report screen.

cards.front: Where do you record a customer payment?
cards.back: Sales > Receive Payment, then apply it against the open invoice(s).

chunks.title: Real transactions: 5 bills, 5 invoices, 5 payments
chunks.bodyHtml:
5 vendor bills (from the Module 05 AP batch) — enter each under Expenses > Bills, coded to the right expense account:

VendorInvoice #Amount
Dominion Aluminum & FastenersDAF-2601-014$3,896.33
Potomac Steel SupplyPSS-2601-001$5,700.30
Commonwealth Powder CoatingCPC-2601-002$1,385.00
MidAtlantic Tool RentalMTR-2601-001$1,411.25
Capital Safety GearCSG-2601-003$875.98

5 customer invoices (from the Module 03 AR data) — enter under Sales > Invoices with VA 5.3% sales tax applied only to taxable goods lines. Example: NOVA FiberBuild Contractors, invoice SMI-2601-001, $15,250.00 + $808.25 tax = $16,058.25, terms Net 30.

5 customer payments — use Receive Payment against those invoices. Include at least one early-payment discount and one short-pay from the AR Payment Remittance Batch so you practice the full range of what actually happens in AR.

chunks.title: Reconcile the bank account
chunks.bodyHtml: Use the January 2026 bank statement to reconcile Operating Checking. Starting balance $132,500.00 should tie to what you entered in Step 3. Walk through matching every transaction and resolving January's three seeded problems — a duplicate deposit, an unrecorded bank fee, and an outstanding check — until the reconciliation difference reads $0.00.

callout.html: This is the exact same reconciliation logic you'll practice in depth in Module 04 — QBO's Reconcile screen just automates part of the matching you'd otherwise do by hand in Excel.

questions.q: Where in QuickBooks Online do you enter a bill from a vendor like Dominion Aluminum & Fasteners?
questions.explanation: Vendor bills (accounts payable) are entered under Expenses > Bills. Sales > Invoices is for customers (accounts receivable) — the opposite direction of money flow.

questions.q: You're setting up Skarion Manufacturing LLC's opening balances. What should the Operating Checking opening balance be?
options: $0.00 — QBO starts every account at zero
questions.explanation: $132,500.00 is Skarion's real Operating Checking balance as of January 1, 2026. $250,000 is the loan payable and $118,400 is opening AR — different accounts entirely.

questions.q: 45% of real entry-level accounting job postings explicitly require experience with which software?
questions.explanation: QuickBooks is the single most-requested accounting software in entry-level postings — which is exactly why this module exists before anything else in the course.

chunks.title: Build it yourself and capture the walkthrough
chunks.instructionsHtml:
Sign up for a free QuickBooks Online trial and follow the full corrected script below end-to-end: company setup, chart of accounts, opening balances, 5 bills, 5 invoices, 5 payments, bank reconciliation, and exporting the P&L and Balance Sheet to Excel. Capture a screenshot at each of the 10 marked steps.

The script also has two required reflection sections built in — fill them out as you go, they become part of your portfolio.
chunks.reflectionPrompt: What was the single most confusing step, and what would you tell a friend to watch out for?
